jobs.purpose: Dispatched on tenancy::TenantProvisioned. Creates the tenant's default Region using the tenant's registered country_code (from tenant.registration_country or the primary billing contact's country). Idempotent — a second dispatch for the same tenant no-ops.
steps: Resolve tenant + verify no existing Region row (idempotency check by (tenant_id) with `is_default=true`).
Resolve country_code — try tenant.registration_country first, then billing contact's country, fall back to `config('region.provisioning.fallback_country_code')` (default 'US').
Look up country defaults in data/country-defaults.json → { currency, timezone, locale, rtl, weekend_days, tax_config }.
Invoke RegionProvisioner with { tenant, country_code, currency, timezone, locale, weekend_days, tax_config, is_default=true, name=country_defaults.name, slug=Str::slug(country_defaults.name) }.
Do NOT consume a region_slot entitlement — the default region is a system-provisioned invariant, not a tenant purchase.
jobs.note: Runs on the notifications queue because it's part of the tenant-onboarding path — its latency is user-visible.

jobs.purpose: Daily nightly at 03:15 UTC. Verifies the invariant `every tenant has exactly one Region row with is_default=true`. Alerts on drift; when configured, auto-heals by re-seeding a default (via SeedDefaultRegionForTenantJob) OR promoting the oldest active Region to default.
steps: SELECT tenant_id, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_default=true) AS default_count FROM regions WHERE deleted_at IS NULL GROUP BY tenant_id HAVING default_count != 1.
For each drifted row: log a telemetry counter + fire the alert per `config('region.reconciliation.alert_on_drift')`.
a. default_count = 0 AND >= 1 active region exists → promote the oldest active Region to is_default=true.
b. default_count = 0 AND no active region exists → dispatch SeedDefaultRegionForTenantJob to provision a fresh one from the tenant's country_code.
c. default_count > 1 → keep the newest is_default row; flip the older ones to is_default=false. Fire RegionDefaultChanged for each demotion.
Write a reconciliation report row to `audit_events`.
jobs.note: Off by default in staging; on in production. Auto-heal is off by default even in production — surface + acknowledge before healing.

jobs.purpose: Daily at 04:15 UTC. Hard-deletes soft-deleted (archived) Region rows past `region.retention.archived_regions_retention_days` (default 730 days — 2 years for financial retention) IF no inbound FK row still references them.
steps: SELECT id, tenant_id FROM regions WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at < NOW() - INTERVAL 'region.retention.archived_regions_retention_days days'.

For each: verify NO inbound row references the Region (branch.region_id, finance.membership.region_id, finance.transaction.region_id, finance.invoice.region_id). If any references remain, SKIP + log — the row survives another day.
When clear: hard-delete the row + fire RegionDeleted with mode='hard'.
The audit row survives per financial retention (7 years) — the audit outlives the Region.
jobs.note: Conservative — the retention window is 730 days, and even then a single lingering inbound FK keeps the row alive indefinitely. Explicit tenant-admin action via `region:archive --force` bypasses the FK check (requires ops sign-off).
